name: Ginger-Almond Florentines
recipeIngredient: 1/4 Cup Firmly packed dark brown sugar
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line two large, heavy baking sheets with aluminum foil and lightly butter the foil.
Combine cream, granulated sugar, brown sugar, and butter in a heavy saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, just until sugars dissolve and butter melts. Add almonds, flour, crystallized ginger, orange zest, and lemon zest. Bring mixture to a boil, stirring constantly. Remove from heat.
Drop 1 tablespoon batter onto prepared baking sheets, spacing cookies about 3 inches apart. Repeat with the second sheet. Bake until deep brown, about 10 minutes.
Remove from oven and slide foil off the sheets. Line the same sheets with new foil and butter again, then repeat with the remaining batter. Cool completely on the foil.
Using a round cookie cutter or glass 3 inches in diameter, push hot cookie edges toward the center to neaten them, shaping into 3-inch rounds. Carefully peel cookies off the foil and arrange smooth-side up on the baking sheets.
For chocolate coating, combine chocolate and shortening in the top of a double boiler. Melt over (not touching) water just until smooth, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat.
Dip one flat side of each biscotti into warm chocolate or spread like icing. Refrigerate until chocolate sets. Makes about 2 dozen biscuits with approximately 3/4 cup chocolate coating.
